steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the function of the underlined portion — 'From which slipped several chirpings / Of chickadee.' — in the text as a whole. Look at what the underlined lines do and how they connect to the rest of the poem.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It implies that the speaker and his friend don't trust each other.: Nothing in the poem suggests distrust. The friends share a wordless glance and commune in silence — the opposite of distrust. ✗
  • Choice B — It reflects a mood of melancholy in the speaker's mind.: The poem's tone is peaceful and harmonious, not melancholic. The silence is comfortable, not sorrowful. ✗
  • Choice C — It demonstrates that the harmony between birds and nature aligns with the tacit understanding between the speaker and his friend.: The bird chirpings slip gently from silence — fitting naturally into the dome of woven leaves — just as the friends communicate through a glance without words. Natural harmony mirrors human harmony. ✓
  • Choice D — It presents a contrasting feeling of the speaker toward nature and his life.: The poem presents no contrast between nature and the speaker's life — both are unified and tranquil throughout. ✗
That is the correct answer.
